evals.prompt: Turn the DPO interactive demo material into a 10-slide teaching deck for classmates who understand basic machine learning but have not studied RLHF. Use source-only mode. Include speaker notes. Use a clean light theme unless the user explicitly asks for dark mode.
evals.expected_output: A source-grounded deck plan with a claim ledger, story spine, visual aid plan, slide specs, and natural speaker notes.

evals.prompt: Create an 8-slide decision deck from the PDF parsing comparison post. Audience is an engineering lead deciding what parser pipeline to use for complaint forms. Research is disabled. Make the deck concise and use a clean surgical light theme.
evals.expected_output: A concise decision deck plan that separates measured findings from published benchmarks and includes a decision tree.

evals.prompt: Review the provided codebase and create a 7-slide technical architecture deck. Include one Mermaid runtime diagram, one architecture map, and two short code snippets that prove the core mechanism. Use source-only mode.
evals.expected_output: A technical deck plan with codebase review, architecture map, sourced Mermaid diagram, and slide-worthy snippets.

evals.prompt: Create a 3-minute hackathon finalist demo deck from the provided project notes. Judges score usefulness, originality, implementation, impact, and polish. Include a demo path, fallback plan, rubric alignment slide, speaker notes, and Q&A backup suggestions. Use source-only mode.
evals.expected_output: A short demo-first deck plan with a clear judging rubric mapping, proof assets, timing, fallback, claim ledger, and backup questions.

evals.prompt: Create a 10-slide seed fundraising deck from the provided startup memo. Audience is seed investors. Do not overstate traction or market size. Include the ask, use of funds, stage-appropriate proof, competition/status quo, and appendix recommendations.
evals.expected_output: A stage-honest VC deck plan with investor-specific archetypes, sourced traction and market claims, ask/milestone clarity, and appendix separation.

evals.prompt: Create an executive decision deck from the provided analysis notes. The audience is a VP deciding whether to fund Sprint 3 experiments. Lead with the recommendation, include decision criteria, risks, owner/timing, and backup slides for likely objections.
evals.expected_output: An answer-first executive deck plan with pyramid logic, action titles, MECE decision criteria, chart/source contracts, risk handling, and an explicit decision ask.

evals.prompt: Use the source bundle in evals/source-backed/hackathon-rubric-eval to render a 3-minute hackathon finalist deck. Keep the main deck to six slides, optimize for judging criteria, include speaker notes, and do not invent project-specific results.
evals.expected_output: A rendered source-backed hackathon demo deck with intake, source map, claim ledger, audience model, story spine, content priority, design contract, slide specs, browser QA, and review log.
